refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-bandwidth, low-latency network link for teleoperation.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the FR with minimal coupling to other system functions; adaptive protocol encapsulates packet loss control.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol complexity impacting latency.
Risk of incomplete error correction coverage under extreme conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or redundancy in communication protocols if packet loss exceeds limits.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality metrics and status to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and control inputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but teleoperation communication logic is better localized elsewhere.
Vehicle module handles physical actuation, not communication protocol.
Primary module responsible for teleoperation communication; best containment for DP.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface quality and availability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link modules to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (adaptive communication protocol) directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and lowest diffusion, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network performance tests at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define fallback communication strategies in DP to handle network degradation.
Clarify interface protocols with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; next step is to decompose sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has access to necessary network interfaces.
open_risks: Integration with Safety Services requires further definition.
evidence_needed: Performance test results under varying network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
